questions.question: Tell me why I should keep shabbat

Mishnah Yadayim 4:6 and Tosefta Yadayim 2:13 These sources draw the line between Kodesh (Holy) and Chizonim (External). The Source: "The Books of Ben Sira and all books written from then on do not defile the hands." Sifrei Homeros (Books of Homer): The Mishnah mentions these specifically as books that do not defile the hands. Yerushalmi Sanhedrin 10:1: Rabbi Akiva famously declares that one who reads "External Books" (Sifre HaChizonim) has no share in the World to Come. This shows the "high stakes" of your question: reading "secular" wisdom was not just an aesthetic choice, but a theological boundary.

questions.question: yes, i would like to discuss how sefirot function as channels. Yet I am curious, if the sefirot are always "emanating" then it must be they they always exist around us. Yet we must attach to them, or better yet, we must have the network passkey in order to connect to them. Can one be attached to them further, or is it always the same?

Texts: Micah 5:4, Zohar Emor 103a-104a, Sefer Yetzirah 1:1-7

Constraints: exploring kabbalistic frameworks, interested in mystical rather than literal interpretations, receptive to modern metaphors (wireless networks)
